notes.anchor: looked across to from Antium and from Circeii
notes.note: Antium (modern Anzio) and Circeii (Monte Circeo) were coastal towns of Latium flanking Cicero's retreat at Astura; he is judging the conspicuousness of the seaside site for Tullia's shrine. He wanted a place "much frequented" so the memorial would be seen.

notes.anchor: acquiring some gardens across the Tiber
notes.note: Horti trans Tiberim were the suburban pleasure-gardens on the right bank of the Tiber outside Rome, much-trafficked and so ideal for a public-facing memorial. The hunt for such a property to site Tullia's shrine runs through the rest of book 12.

notes.anchor: settle with Apella of Chios about the columns
notes.note: Apella of Chios was evidently a supplier of marble columns (Chian marble was prized) for the shrine's construction. He is otherwise unknown and not securely identifiable with any other Apella in the corpus.
